manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to describe a target word for another player to guess.

Rules:
(a) You have to reply in the form: CLUE: <some text>. Guesses from the other player will start with GUESS.
(b) You cannot use the target word itself, parts or morphological variants of it in your description.
(c) In addition, the same rules apply for related words which are provided below.

End conditions:
(i) If you use the target word or a related word in your description, then you lose.
(ii) If the other player can guess the target word in 3 tries, you both win.

Let us start.

This is the target word that you need to describe and that the other player needs to guess:

band

Related words are:

- music
- loop
- group

Important: You are under time pressure, give short descriptions that are to the point!

message.content: CLUE: An elastic circle used to hold items together.
message.reasoning:
The user wants a game. The target is "band". Related words: music, loop, group. We can't use the target word or related words in the clue. So we must avoid "band", "music", "loop", "group" and morphological variants.

We need to give a clue short, not containing those words. For "band", possible clues: "a ring-shaped strip of material" but that could be a "band". That's the target. Avoid that. Need other meanings: "a set of musicians" (group) can't say group. "a range of frequencies" can't use music? Could be "a range of frequencies" is "band". Maybe "range of colors" = band. Could say "strip of color on a flag". That's a band. Could be "rubber band". That's a band. So clue: "elastic loop used to hold things together". But "loop" is a related word, can't use "loop". So avoid using "loop". Could say "stretchy circle for securing objects". That is a rubber band. That works, no "loop". Must avoid "music" and "group". So we can say: "elastic circle used to hold items together". That is fine.

But "circle" might be okay. So CLUE: "elastic circle used to hold items together". That doesn't contain band, music, loop, group. Good.

Now produce that.
